text: {"claims":[{"statement":"In 2003, a rumor that the oral polio vaccine contained porcine material and sterilizing agents led to a boycott of polio vaccination in Northern Nigeria.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In 2003, a rumor that the oral polio vaccine contained porcine material and sterilizing agents led to a boycott of polio vaccination in Northern Nigeria.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that the rumor caused a boycott of polio vaccination in Northern Nigeria.","population":"Northern Nigeria","intervention":"rumor that oral polio vaccine contained porcine material and sterilizing agents","comparator":"","outcome":"boycott of polio vaccination","timeframe":"2003","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Following the vaccine boycott, an upsurge in polio cases resulted in wild-polio re-infection in 20 countries across Africa and Asia.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Following this vaccine boycott, an upsurge in polio cases resulted in wild-polio re-infection in 20 countries across Africa and Asia","entailment":"The sentence links the boycott to a subsequent increase in polio cases that spread to 20 countries, indicating a causal relationship.","population":"20 countries across Africa and Asia","intervention":"vaccine boycott","comparator":"","outcome":"wild-polio re-infection","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"In 2017, a rumor that the Nigerian military was “vaccinating” school children with the monkeypox virus led panicked parents to withdraw their children from school and refuse vaccination.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In 2017, another rumor broke out in the country’s Southeastern part, purporting that the Nigerian military was “vaccinating” school children with the monkeypox virus [12]. This false story led panicked parents to withdraw their children from school and, uncharacteristically, refuse to take them for vaccination.","entailment":"The quoted passage describes the rumor and its direct effect of causing parents to withdraw children from school and refuse vaccination.","population":"parents of school children in the country’s Southeastern part","intervention":"rumor that military was vaccinating children with monkeypox virus","comparator":"","outcome":"withdrawal of children from school and refusal of vaccination","timeframe":"2017","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Vaccination rates dropped in the southeastern region after the 2017 rumor, a region that previously had some of the highest vaccine uptake rates in the country.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Soon after that, vaccination rates dropped in that part of the country – a region that previously had some of the highest vaccine uptake rates in the country.","entailment":"The sentence indicates a temporal association where vaccination rates fell following the rumor, implying a causal link.","population":"southeastern region of Nigeria","intervention":"rumor about monkeypox vaccination","comparator":"","outcome":"drop in vaccination rates","timeframe":"shortly after 2017 rumor","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Early vaccine procurement, domestic production of vaccines, the severity of the pandemic, a country’s health infrastructure, and vaccine acceptance are significant determinants of COVID-19 vaccine coverage.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Early vaccine procurement, domestic production of vaccines, the severity of the pandemic, a country’s health infrastructure, and vaccine acceptance are significant determinants of COVID-19 vaccine coverage","entailment":"The quoted list explicitly names factors that determine COVID-19 vaccine coverage, defining them as determinants.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"COVID-19 vaccine coverage","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Widespread vaccine acceptance is crucial for achieving sufficient immunization coverage.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"However, evidence has shown that widespread vaccine acceptance is crucial for achieving sufficient immunization coverage","entailment":"The sentence reports evidence that links high vaccine acceptance to sufficient immunization coverage, indicating an empirical relationship.","population":"","intervention":"widespread vaccine acceptance","comparator":"","outcome":"sufficient immunization coverage","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Nigeria introduced COVID-19 vaccines with barely enough doses to cover 1% of the population, with vaccine demand far outstripping vaccine supply.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Thus, like other LMICs, Nigeria introduced COVID-19 vaccines with barely enough doses to cover 1% of the population, with vaccine demand far outstripping vaccine supply.","entailment":"The quoted statement provides factual information about the proportion of the population that could be covered and the mismatch between demand and supply.","population":"Nigeria","intervention":"introduction of COVID-19 vaccines","comparator":"","outcome":"coverage of 1% of population; demand exceeds supply","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"barely enough doses to cover 1%","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"As the global supply of COVID-19 vaccines improves in the second half of 2021, demand and acceptance, along with absorption capacity, will become the top coverage drivers.","claimType":"predictive","quote":"As the global supply of COVID-19 vaccines improves in the second half of 2021 and more doses become available and deployed in LMICs, demand and acceptance, along with absorption capacity, will become the top coverage drivers.","entailment":"The sentence projects that once supply improves, demand, acceptance, and absorption capacity will drive coverage, indicating a predictive claim.","population":"LMICs","intervention":"improved global supply of COVID-19 vaccines","comparator":"","outcome":"demand, acceptance, and absorption capacity become top coverage drivers","timeframe":"second half of 2021","quantifiers":"","modality":"predictive"},{"statement":"Nigeria received 40 million COVID-19 vaccine doses in August 2021 and commenced the second vaccination phase.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For example, Nigeria received 40 million COVID-19 vaccine doses in August 2021 and commenced the second vaccination phase","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports the factual receipt of doses and the start of a vaccination phase.","population":"Nigeria","intervention":"receipt of 40 million COVID-19 vaccine doses","comparator":"","outcome":"commencement of second vaccination phase","timeframe":"August 2021","quantifiers":"40 million doses","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Misinformation is defined as false or inaccurate information or communication.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"We use the term misinformation to denote false or inaccurate information or communication","entailment":"The sentence provides a definition of the term misinformation.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Conspiracy theories are a particular kind in which the creators of conspiracy theories attribute the root causes of events or trends to shadowy networks or cabals carrying out secret plots to control, undermine or exploit the public, governments, or institutions","entailment":"The passage defines conspiracy theories by describing their characteristic attribution to secret plots.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The study was designed to systematically elicit narratives","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"At the time of data collection, COVID-19 vaccines had not yet been deployed in
